Prefer 部品 in ordinary electronics and hardware prose. Use 電子部品 when electronics context must be explicit. Avoid コンポーネント unless software/system-architecture context requires it.
Use when electronics context must be made explicit; otherwise 部品 is sufficient.

Use when solder does not spread or bond properly to the metal surface.

note: Use in practical soldering explanations for solder that flows and bonds cleanly.
Not suitable for electronics. Avoid 配管はんだ付け用のはんだ (verbose literal).
Often acid flux; corrodes PCBs and components. Avoid 配管はんだ付け用のフラックス.
Corrodes boards and parts; must not be used in electronics soldering. See corrosion entry.
Use 腐食 for acid-flux damage to boards and parts. Do not use 侵食 in this context.
For acid flux damaging boards or parts. Avoid 侵食する here.
DuPont heat-resistant polyimide tape. Use for masking/protecting nearby areas from heat or molten solder. Do not imply direct iron-tip contact is safe.
Use official spelling when referring to the DuPont material by name. Generic term: カプトンテープ.
For protecting pads/areas from solder: はんだを付けたくない場所をマスクする. Avoid bare はんだをかけたくない.

note: Deliberate pre-applied solder on a pad before placing a component: use はんだを盛る (verb) / 盛ったはんだ (noun). Avoid はんだブロブ. Avoid はんだボール in this context. 予備はんだ only if source clearly means pre-tinning.
In soldering prose: do not transliterate as ブロブ. Use 盛ったはんだ (pre-applied solder on pad) or はんだの塊 (solder mass) depending on context.

ja_term: 予備はんだする／あらかじめはんだを盛る
note: Use only when the process clearly means pre-tinning a surface or lead. Context-dependent.

ja_term: 予備はんだ済み／あらかじめはんだを盛った
note: Context-dependent. See pre-tin entry.
Use only for actual detached spherical solder balls, BGA balls, or failure-mode solder balls. Not for a deliberate solder blob placed on a pad before component placement.
Solder ball on BGA package underside. Use はんだボール only for actual BGA balls, not for a deliberate pad preload.

Japanese term for a poor solder joint (cold joint / insufficient wetting). Use only when source context is about a defective joint or poor wetting. Do not apply to deliberate solder-on-pad operations.
Japanese term for solder sitting on the surface without proper wetting (appears bonded but is not). Use only when context matches a bad joint. Do not apply to deliberate solder-on-pad operations.

ja_term: フラックス入りはんだ
note: General and modern term. Avoid フラックスコア入りはんだ (verbose). For the circular phrase フラックスコア内のフラックス, rephrase: フラックス入りはんだに含まれるフラックスだけでは.

ja_term: ヤニ入りはんだ／ロジン入りはんだ
note: ヤニ入りはんだ is familiar in older Japanese electronics prose; use フラックス入りはんだ if context is general or modern.
Use 基板 (板 = board/plate). Do NOT use 基盤 (盤 = disc/foundation). Common kanji error. PCB → 基板; printed circuit board → プリント基板.

ja_term: 半日で作れる／午後の半日で作れる／一日かからず作れる
note: Do not translate literally as 午後一つで作れる. Choose depending on tone and context.

For netlist, schematic, or PCB connection-count checks. Avoid コンポーネント数 here; that rendering is only for software-architecture or formal tool-terminology contexts. Does not weaken the general component → 部品 rule.

ja_term: 抵抗成分による発熱／抵抗による発熱
note: PCB trace/current context (unwanted heating from trace resistance). Avoid 抵抗加熱, which reads as an intentional heating method. ジュール熱 acceptable if natural; do not over-formalize casual prose.

ja_term: 蛇行配線／サーペンタイン配線
note: PCB length-matching trace pattern. First explanatory mention: 蛇行させた配線パターン（サーペンタイン配線、ミアンダ配線）or concise equivalent. Avoid leaving bare カタカナ without the 蛇行配線 function gloss.
Synonym/variant of serpentine trace in PCB length-matching context. See serpentine trace entry for first-mention pattern.
Prefer 溶けたはんだ for "liquid solder"/"molten solder" in hand-soldering prose. Avoid 液体はんだ when it sounds unnatural.

ja_term: 洗浄／洗い流す／除去する
note: For removing flux residue after soldering. Avoid クリーンアップ.
For steam/trapped-water damage inside components (e.g. popcorning). Prefer 破裂 over 爆発 unless the source clearly means an explosion.

ja_term: 煙を吸い出す／煙を逃がす／吸い込まないようにする
note: For fan/ventilation context clearing flux fumes. Avoid 吹き飛ばす, which implies blowing debris rather than venting smoke.

ja_term: 設計ルールチェッカー（DRC）／設計ルールチェック（DRC）
note: Tool → 設計ルールチェッカー（DRC）; process/action → 設計ルールチェック（DRC）. After first use, DRC alone is fine.
Avoid bare ボードレイアウト.
Avoid bare ボードレイアウトツール.

ja_term: 配線／配線パターン／配線経路／配線層
note: Context-dependent noun rendering of the completed wiring/path. See TRANSLATION_GUIDE.md for the grammar-based routing/route rule.

ja_term: 配線作業／配線する／配線を引く／引き回す
note: Gerund/activity rendering. First use in CAD/process explanation may be 配線（ルーティング）; avoid overusing standalone ルーティング after that.
Standard PCB workflow phrase pairing.

ja_term: 配線ツール／自動配線ツール
note: 自動配線ツール for auto-routing tools specifically; 配線ツール more general.
